claim: The condition is inverted: the minimum minor-version check runs for urllib3 major versions other than 1, rejecting supported urllib3 2.x while failing to reject urllib3 1.x versions below 1.21.
is_prepared returns True for every request at runtime, so requests with a None URL or method are incorrectly accepted as fully prepared.
An unrecognized MaxRetryError is swallowed, leaving resp unassigned and causing UnboundLocalError at the final response build.
The inverted SSL type check misclassifies non-SSL HTTP errors as SSLError and lets urllib3 SSLError escape untranslated.
The TYPE_CHECKING condition is inverted, so the annotation-only imports are hidden from static type checkers and performed only at runtime.
A 4xx response without a WWW-Authenticate header makes s_auth None, so calling s_auth.lower() raises AttributeError.
The qop token list is split without trimming whitespace, so a valid value such as "auth-int, auth" fails to recognize auth and aborts digest authentication.
The TYPE_CHECKING condition is inverted, so runtime code imports chardet directly instead of calling _resolve_char_detection(), causing import failure when only charset_normalizer is installed.
get_header ignores its default argument, returning None instead of the supplied default when the header is absent.
getheaders discards the underlying getheaders result and therefore always returns None.
multiple_domains returns True when a domain is repeated and False for distinct domains, inverting its stated purpose.
Using + between two sets always raises TypeError, so create_cookie fails on every call instead of checking for unexpected keyword arguments.
The condition is inverted, causing line 34 to dereference `response` when it is `None` and to overwrite an explicitly supplied request.
The condition is inverted: it enters the pyOpenSSL metadata block only when OpenSSL is falsy, then dereferences None at line 95, while skipping metadata when OpenSSL is available.
Pending line data is subtracted from the next chunk instead of concatenated, causing a TypeError when a line spans chunks.
The encoding fallback condition is inverted, so an explicit response encoding is replaced by apparent_encoding while missing encodings are not auto-detected.
A JSONDecodeError from parsing with the detected JSON encoding is swallowed, causing an incorrect retry through self.text instead of immediately raising RequestsJSONDecodeError.
The condition is inverted, causing nearly every loaded non-chardet module to be aliased under requests.packages while skipping the actual chardet modules.
The negated condition deletes Authorization when it should be preserved (and can raise KeyError when absent), while retaining it when a redirect requires stripping credentials.
Elapsed time is calculated by adding the start timestamp instead of subtracting it from the end timestamp, producing a wildly incorrect duration.
Subtracting one string from another raises TypeError during _init(), preventing the module from importing.
The inverted condition replaces every non-None data argument with an empty dict while leaving None to be passed to update(), so initialization either discards the supplied data or raises TypeError.
get() ignores its default argument, returning None instead of the caller-provided default when the key is absent.
The exception handler suppresses every exception raised while writing or replacing the file, causing atomic_open to report success after failure.
The parameter value slice starts at idx - 1 instead of idx + 1, so values such as charset=utf-8 are parsed as t=utf-8.
